description: Merge conflict resolved at tier auto-resolve. Branch: overstory/build-cache-fix/behavior-tree-ist-5aac. Agent: build-cache-fix. Conflicting files: .mulch/mulch.config.yaml.

name: pure-state-updater
description: State updater functions must be pure — no side effects (localStorage, fetch, etc.) inside setState(prev => ...). Move side effects to useEffect that depends on the state value.

name: displayState-dedup
description: BehaviourTree caches last JSON.stringify(state) per nodeId; emits state in TickTraceEvent only when serialized value changes. TickStore.getSnapshotAtTick fills missing state via getLastNodeState(nodeId, tickId) (carry-forward). Avoids wire traffic for unchanged node states.

title: WHI-10 mutable trees runtime design
rationale: Owner answered all 8 open questions from research phase

name: bidirectional-ring-buffer
description: RingBuffer unshift/unshiftMany prepend to oldest end, evict from newest end. When batch >= capacity, keeps newest capacity items (mirroring pushMany). TickStore.insertBefore uses unshiftMany; evicted are always previously-stored ticks (not batch overflows). TreeInspector.insertTicksBefore does NOT increment totalTickCount — historical loads are separate from live tick count.

name: use-tick-poller-windowed
description: useTickPoller signature: (selection, pollRateMs, windowSize, mode: 'streaming'|'windowed'). Returns {ticks, latestBackwardTicks, isLoading, error, fetchBefore, seekToRange}. streaming mode auto-polls forward; windowed mode only fetches on explicit fetchBefore/seekToRange calls. fetchBefore prepends + evicts newest; seekToRange replaces window. use-studio-controls.ts needs update when integrating (breaking API change from TickRecord[]).

content: Windowed time travel UI: StudioControls exposes tickBounds (StudioTickBounds={minTickId,maxTickId,totalCount}), onFetchTicksAround(tickId), isLoadingWindow. useTimeTravelControls accepts options.{onNeedTick,serverBounds,isLoading}. TimelinePanel uses serverBounds for scrubber min/max and shows 'Loaded: N/M ticks' text. BehaviourTreeDebugger wires: handleNeedTick→studioControls.onFetchTicksAround. use-studio-controls polls ticks.bounds every 5s and implements onFetchTicksAround via ticks.range fetch merged into extraTicks state.

useInspector bidirectional insert: tracks ingestedTickIdsRef as Set<number> (not lastIngestedTickId scalar). Forward ticks (tickId > newestStored) go via ingestTicks(). Backward ticks (tickId <= newestStored) go via inspector.insertTicks?.(records) optional-chained — no-op until data-layer builder adds TreeInspector.insertTicks().

StudioUiSettings windowed fields: windowSize (default 1000, target ticks loaded for time travel) and fetchBatchSize (default 1000, ticks per windowed request). Both persisted in localStorage via use-ui-settings.ts. SettingsPanel renders both as NumberField with min=100.

Profiler backward tick ingestion: ingestTicks() guards on lastProcessedTickId blocking historical ticks. Use ingestTicksBefore() which guards on tickContribByTick.has(tickId) instead. TreeInspector.insertTicksBefore must call profiler.ingestTicksBefore() not ingestTicks().

name: whi14-sequential-leads
description: WHI-14 extended time travel: 2-phase implementation — server retention (ticks.bounds, ticks.range, ticks.queryBefore) merged first, then UI layer (bidirectional TickStore, windowed useTickPoller, TimelinePanel window controls). Reconciliation builder needed when 2 builders have API contract mismatches.

name: no-isLoading-in-background-polls
description: fetchForward background poll must NOT call setIsLoading(true/false) — only user-initiated operations (fetchBefore, seekToRange) should toggle isLoading. Background polls toggling isLoading caused blinking hourglass + button flicker every poll cycle.

name: window-selector-no-auto-fetch
description: stepForward/stepBack in useTimeTravelControls stop at loaded-window boundary without auto-fetching; user explicitly moves window via TimelinePanel window selector slider (onSelectWindow -> onFetchTicksAround). Scrubber range now spans loaded window only; window-selector range spans full server bounds.

name: range-trimmer-popup
description: iOS-style range trimmer: WindowRangeTrimmer uses dual pointer-captured handles on a custom div track (not two HTML range inputs). fromFrac/toFrac state [0,1] mapped to tick IDs. Popup anchored via position:absolute on .bt-timeline__window-selector; button opens/closes via trimmerOpen state. onSelectRange(from,to) replaces old onSelectWindow(tickId) — caller does no range computation.

name: range-trimmer-apply-flow
description: Apply calls onSelectRange(from,to) then controls.goToTick(from) in TimelinePanel.handleTrimmerApply. onFetchTickRange sets manualWindowActive=true (blocks streaming poll overwrite); onResumeStreaming resets it when paused->live transition detected via prevModeRef in BehaviourTreeDebugger. windowSize removed from StudioUiSettings; seekToRange uses Math.min(to-from+1, 10000) dynamic limit; rolling cap uses ringBufferSize.

name: range-trimmer-draggable
description: WindowRangeTrimmer selection div: handleSelectionPointerDown uses setPointerCapture, records startFrac/startFrom/startTo, on pointermove shifts both fromFrac+toFrac by delta maintaining span width, clamps to [0,1-width]. CSS: pointer-events none→auto, cursor:grab/grabbing. In tests: must mock element.setPointerCapture=vi.fn() since jsdom lacks it.

name: seek-clears-inspector
description: useInspector detects window replacement (seekToRange) when ingested.size>0 and none of current ticks appear in ingestedTickIdsRef; calls inspector.clearTicks() before ingesting to prevent OLD+NEW accumulation in scrubber range. pausedInspector is refreshed via isLoadingWindow false-transition effect in BehaviourTreeDebugger.
